title: The Fight

jokes.text: Sounds like you took a snake and twisted it like a rag until its blood and guts came out.
jokes.setup: Tom describes his 'Kahlua-style' Snake Juice liqueur
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disgusting imagery of what the name 'Snake Juice' actually sounds like
jokes.explanation: Excellent visceral imagery that perfectly captures how unappetizing the name sounds. Great economy and specificity.

jokes.text: I mix a bunch of alcohol together, I add some sugar and coffee and some other junk, and it kind of tastes like Kahlua.
jokes.setup: Asked what 'Kahlua-style' means
jokes.punchline: Tom's hilariously unprofessional description of his 'high-end' product
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Tom's entrepreneurial delusions vs reality

jokes.text: Done-zo list explanation and escalation
jokes.setup: What's a Done-zo list?
jokes.punchline: Tom's elaborate explanation building to the absurd 'No, we're done-zo' Putt-Putt scenario
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure with perfect character voice. 'Done-zo' becomes quotable catchphrase.

jokes.text: I just... I couldn't deal with his face.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about Ann's breakup with Jesse
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest and shallow reason for ending relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and brutal honesty. Subverts expectation of deeper relationship issues.

jokes.text: Oh, well, I'm technically out of the country. So I would have to call him from a weird number.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about getting a book back from the ex
jokes.punchline: Ann's ridiculous excuse to avoid normal human interaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's conflict avoidance in absurd way

jokes.text: He's a triple Pisces.
jokes.setup: Ann describing her new boyfriend Mattias
jokes.punchline: Impossible astrological classification delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Short and absurd. Shows character's flaky side with impossible astrology.

jokes.text: Jerry, you'll be playing a boring beer drinker. Your name will be Jerry.
jokes.setup: Tom assigning guerrilla marketing roles
jokes.punchline: Jerry gets to play himself, highlighting his boring reputation
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta joke that's mean but fits the show's treatment of Jerry perfectly.

jokes.text: 'Ca-razy.' Like 'crazy.'
jokes.setup: Ron mispronouncing Tom's made-up word 'K-razzy'
jokes.punchline: Tom's condescending explanation of his own terrible slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's pretentious side and failed slang

jokes.text: My only official recommendations are U.S. Army-issued mustache trimmers, Morton Salt, and the C.R. Laurence Fein two-inch, axe-style scraper oscillating knife blade.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining he won't endorse products he doesn't believe in
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific and manly product recommendations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson character comedy. Incredibly specific and manly, delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Series of Dennis Cooper's revenge signs about his wife's affair
jokes.setup: Dennis Cooper went 'bananas' after his wife's affair
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and pathetic public signs through City Hall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from official health department tone to desperate personal pleas. 'Mayor of Whoreville' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: The Department of Health congratulates Jan Cooper. Miss Chlamydia.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Dennis Cooper's revenge signs
jokes.punchline: Treating STD infection like a beauty pageant title
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from the previous sign, treating a disease like an award. Perfect economy of language.

jokes.text: Yes, I know. I couldn't get it earlier.
jokes.setup: Ann reacts with 'Wow' to 9 AM interview time
jokes.punchline: Leslie thinks Ann is impressed, not dismayed by the early time
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Leslie's obliviousness to normal people's schedules

jokes.text: Because I'm almost done with it, Ann. And I want to talk to you about Patty.
jokes.setup: Ann asks why she needs to read Freedom by Jonathan Franzen
jokes.punchline: Leslie's completely self-centered reason for homework assignment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's self-absorption masked as helpfulness

jokes.text: It happened once. Just once. That already is too many times.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying pulling things from butts doesn't happen often
jokes.punchline: Ann's traumatized reaction to single incident
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and relatable sentiment about traumatic work experiences

jokes.text: That makes it sexy. / Can I use a weird voice and try to freak people out?
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting role-playing to make the task fun
jokes.punchline: April's completely different and disturbing interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast and misdirection. Shows April's dark creativity.

jokes.text: 'K' to the 'N' to the 'O-P-E,' she's the dopest little shorty in all Pawnee, Indiana. / Why didn't you just stop at 'Pawnee'?
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio's attempt at a rap about Leslie
jokes.punchline: Leslie's practical critique of his rhyme scheme failure
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's practical nature vs Jean-Ralphio's style over substance

jokes.text: She might be up all night, but I think someone else is gonna be doing the cramming. Uh-oh!
jokes.setup: Leslie mentioning Ann will be cramming for interview
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's inappropriate sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual pun but fits Jean-Ralphio's character perfectly

jokes.text: Is there enough room for some 'man-aise' in this lady sandwich?
jokes.setup: The Douche approaching Leslie and Ann
jokes.punchline: Horrible portmanteau pickup line
jokes.explanation: Intentionally awful pickup line that perfectly captures the character's douchiness

jokes.text: Wait! Were you on the show where we had that stripper do math? Classic, right?
jokes.setup: The Douche recognizing Leslie from his radio show
jokes.punchline: His proudest professional moment is exploitative and absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the character's lowbrow humor and self-satisfaction

jokes.text: Reaction beat - Leslie's silence after 'Classic, right?'
jokes.setup: The Douche expecting Leslie to agree his stripper math bit was 'classic'
jokes.punchline: Leslie's uncomfortable silence speaks volumes
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of silence to show Leslie's horror. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Janet Snakehole and Bert Macklin introduction sequence
jokes.setup: April and Andy doing their role-playing personas
jokes.punchline: Elaborate backstories for simple guerrilla marketing task
jokes.explanation: Iconic character creation that became fan favorite. Perfect April/Andy dynamic.

jokes.text: I got to admit, I thought your costume would maybe be a little bit sluttier.
jokes.setup: Tom commenting on April's Janet Snakehole costume
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate and creepy comment delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringey for Tom's character. Shows his inappropriate side.

jokes.text: Tom's rapid-fire terrible business ideas list
jokes.setup: Tom trying to convince Ron to invest
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly ridiculous business concepts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurd ideas. 'Eclipse' nightclub concept is comedy gold. Perfect Tom character moment.

jokes.text: If you don't, I'll shave Jean-Ralphio's head. / Yeah, I'd like to see that.
jokes.setup: Tom offering Ron a deal to try Snake Juice
jokes.punchline: Ron's interest in the punishment rather than the product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - more interested in someone else's suffering than helping Tom

jokes.text: Damn, if that isn't delicious.
jokes.setup: Ron reluctantly tries Snake Juice expecting it to be terrible
jokes.punchline: His genuine surprise and approval
jokes.explanation: Misdirection works well - we expect Ron to hate it. His genuine approval gives Tom a rare win and sets up the drinking chaos to follow.

jokes.text: If you're looking for douches, they're in Aisle Me.
jokes.setup: The Douche explaining his pickup line
jokes.punchline: Self-referential pun about his own nickname
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly fits the character's self-awareness about being terrible

jokes.text: I don't know. He's dumb, but he's fun. / Thank you.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how long Ann's relationship will last
jokes.punchline: Ann's blunt assessment while he's sitting right there
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward honesty and his oblivious 'thank you' response

jokes.text: Well, this hat is an idiot.
jokes.setup: Ann's metaphor about trying on different relationship 'hats'
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment of the current 'hat'
jokes.explanation: Great use of Ann's metaphor turned against her. Perfect Leslie bluntness.

jokes.text: Ron's Snake Juice endorsement speech
jokes.setup: Ron reluctantly agrees to endorse Snake Juice
jokes.punchline: His overly formal, sincere endorsement of terrible alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - treating ridiculous task with complete seriousness

jokes.text: It's 140 proof, which means it's 70% alcohol. But don't worry. There's plenty of caffeine in it to keep you awake.
jokes.setup: Tom promoting Snake Juice to Chris
jokes.punchline: Describing a drink that could literally kill someone as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about dangerous alcohol marketed as benefit. Perfect Tom obliviousness.

jokes.text: I believe an ounce of that would literally kill me.
jokes.setup: Tom describing the 140-proof Snake Juice
jokes.punchline: Chris's matter-of-fact assessment of its lethality
jokes.explanation: Perfect health-conscious Chris response delivered with his characteristic earnestness

jokes.text: Leslie and Ann's escalating drunk fight
jokes.setup: Both women getting increasingly drunk and hostile
jokes.punchline: Their friendship deteriorating into petty insults
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how alcohol reveals underlying tensions

jokes.text: You're stupid, and you're drunk and you're stupid.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to stay calm during the fight
jokes.punchline: Her immediate descent into childish name-calling
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Leslie's stated intentions and actual behavior

jokes.text: You don't even know one thing. I didn't even say one thing. And then she asked me the whole thing, and I didn't even do it once.
jokes.setup: Leslie completely drunk and incoherent
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness nonsense that sounds important to her
jokes.explanation: Good drunk logic that captures how important nonsense feels when intoxicated

jokes.text: I'm like an elephant, okay? If I walk into a room, it's like, okay, he's in there.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to make a profound drunk statement
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical metaphor about his presence
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Andy logic. The tautological nature makes it brilliantly stupid.

jokes.text: Bababooey.
jokes.setup: Andy's elephant metaphor
jokes.punchline: Random Howard Stern reference as punctuation
jokes.explanation: Random but perfectly placed. Shows Andy's pop culture brain even when drunk.

jokes.text: Farts and poop and love and stuff / Macaroni salad
jokes.setup: Andy singing while drunk
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness lyrics ending with random food
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk Andy song. Childish topics building to random food reference.

jokes.text: Your mother's butt.
jokes.setup: April asking where to drive first
jokes.punchline: Andy's juvenile response to practical question
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but perfectly fits drunk Andy's mental state

jokes.text: I'm so alone.
jokes.setup: Everyone loaded into April's car
jokes.punchline: Jerry's existential crisis while drunk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - pathetic and sad even when surrounded by friends

jokes.text: Really? / No, I threw up in the shower.
jokes.setup: Chris claiming he ran 5k while hungover
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction revealing he's as sick as everyone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character moment. Chris can't maintain his facade.

jokes.text: First, you take the cow to the killing floor...
jokes.setup: Ron explaining the burger-making process
jokes.punchline: Starting with graphic slaughter details to hungover people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron obliviousness to others' discomfort. Purely in character.

jokes.text: Then our heads will explode, and we will die.
jokes.setup: Interview candidate talking
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic overstatement of her hangover
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie dramatics. Shows her tendency to catastrophize.

jokes.text: I owe her, like, a million apologies. I think I owe you one, too. I'm sorry. For what? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Leslie feels guilty about her fight with Ann
jokes.punchline: Her drunk logic extending to apologizing to April for unknown reasons
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character work - her people-pleasing nature extended to absurd levels while hungover.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. For what? / I don't know.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to apologize to Ben
jokes.punchline: She doesn't know what she's apologizing for
jokes.explanation: Perfect hungover guilt behavior. Relatable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. For what? I don't know. Are you okay? No, I'm sad.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizes to Ben without knowing why
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission of not knowing what she's apologizing for, followed by simple sadness
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Leslie's hungover emotional state. The simplicity of the exchange and her honest confusion work well.

jokes.text: Bert Macklin may be dead, but I'm his brother Kip Hackman. Why wouldn't you have his same last name? Shut up, Kyle.
jokes.setup: Andy introduces a new character as Bert Macklin's brother
jokes.punchline: Kyle's logical question about the different last names and Andy's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Great callback that develops the Bert Macklin mythology. Kyle pointing out the logical flaw and Andy's annoyed dismissal is perfectly written.

jokes.text: You know what? Snitches get stitches.
jokes.setup: Donna revealing she also owns shares Tom had to sell
jokes.punchline: Tom's empty threat using playground logic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's powerless frustration

jokes.text: Snitches get stitches.
jokes.setup: Tom asks why Donna doesn't have to sell her club shares
jokes.punchline: Donna's matter-of-fact threat delivered in her calm manner
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - using street terminology with her characteristic calm delivery. The contrast between the threat and her demeanor makes it funny.

jokes.text: It's like when Vin did Boiler Room. That's what led to him being xXx and doing Furious.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his government strategy
jokes.punchline: Using Vin Diesel's career as a business model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom reference and flawed logic. Shows his pop culture obsession.

jokes.text: You're wearing snow pants.
jokes.setup: Ben visiting hungover Leslie
jokes.punchline: Leslie's inexplicable clothing choice
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag and random detail. Perfect hangover confusion.

jokes.text: I got home last night, and I thought I might go sledding.
jokes.setup: Ben questioning the snow pants
jokes.punchline: Leslie's drunk logic from the night before
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk Leslie logic. Shows her optimistic but impractical nature.

jokes.text: I can't believe that Leslie and I got drunk in a bar and fought about boys. We're so much better than that.
jokes.setup: Ann reflects on the previous night
jokes.punchline: Her realization of how cliché their behavior was
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness about falling into stereotypical behavior patterns.

jokes.text: What the hell is in Snake Juice, Demerol?
jokes.setup: Everyone feeling terrible after drinking Snake Juice
jokes.punchline: Ann's medical professional assessment of the hangover
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Snake Juice's toxicity with Ann's medical perspective

jokes.text: Just rent a limo, ask her to the prom. I'm sure she'll say yes.
jokes.setup: Ben asking about Leslie's feelings
jokes.punchline: Ann reducing complex adult relationship to high school terms
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the high school comparison from earlier scene

jokes.text: Just rent a limo, ask her to the prom. I'm sure she'll say yes.
jokes.setup: Ben is being awkward about his feelings for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Ann's sarcastic reduction of their situation to high school terms
jokes.explanation: Good observation about their relationship dynamic. Ann's sarcasm cuts through the romantic tension effectively.

jokes.text: Jan Cooper was a terrible wife. I just want everybody to know that.
jokes.setup: Dennis Cooper appearing at the interview
jokes.punchline: Still obsessing over his personal problems
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing he hasn't moved on from his obsession

jokes.text: Your sweater's on inside out. And backwards.
jokes.setup: Ann arriving for her interview
jokes.punchline: Multiple clothing mistakes showing her state
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of clothing mistakes. Shows hangover state clearly.

jokes.text: That should be the official slogan for Snake Juice.
jokes.setup: Ann mentioning 'regret and shame'
jokes.punchline: Perfect anti-marketing slogan for Tom's product
jokes.explanation: Great callback that perfectly summarizes the episode's consequences

jokes.text: But first, I am gonna go throw up in a wastebasket. Would you mind if I joined you? Not at all. Shall we?
jokes.setup: Ann needs to throw up during her interview
jokes.punchline: Leslie politely asking to join her and their formal approach to mutual vomiting
jokes.explanation: Excellent friendship moment where their politeness extends even to shared nausea. The formal 'Shall we?' makes it funnier and shows their bond.

jokes.text: Friends, waffles, and work. Or waffles, friends, work. It doesn't matter. But work is third.
jokes.setup: Leslie reflecting on life priorities
jokes.punchline: Her characteristic overthinking of a simple concept, but landing on perfect truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment and life philosophy. Became iconic quote that captures the show's heart.

jokes.text: Hello? Did you wet your whistle? I wish. Me likey. Who knows? She's probably a lesbian. They were in the bathroom a long time.
jokes.setup: The Douche tells his radio story about the night
jokes.punchline: The increasingly crude and incorrect assumptions about Leslie and Ann
jokes.explanation: Ends the episode on The Douche's characteristic crudeness and complete misunderstanding of the situation. Fits his character as the show's intentionally terrible radio DJ.

jokes.text: The Douche's radio show recap and reactions
jokes.setup: The Douche telling his radio partner about the club
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of what happened and sexist conclusions
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing The Douche's complete obliviousness

tmdb_overview: When Leslie tries to get Ann a job at city hall, her lukewarm reaction causes their first-ever fight. Tom invites the entire department to the Snakehole Lounge to help him promote his new alcohol, "Snake Juice."
insight: Escalation-driven character comedy sustains 62 jokes across tense group dynamics with minimal setup.
